mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 255 despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production (INDPRO) growth indicates robust corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through improved cash flow expectations.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high INDPRO growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) signals strong aggregate demand, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through sectoral demand synergies.
failure_condition: SPX returns show no significant correlation with combined HOUST/RSAFS trends, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A labor market tightening (UNRATE < 3.5%) reduces corporate labor costs and increases consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during late-cycle regimes.
failure_condition: SPX returns in low UNRATE regimes show negative or neutral correlation with labor market tightness, or 20d returns in such periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Weak GDP growth (GDPC1) below 1.5% annualized signals economic stagnation, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors discount lower corporate earnings.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where GDPC1 falls below 1.5 despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A flattening yield curve (FEDFUNDS - 10Y Treasury) predicts recession risks, reducing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in flattening yield curve environments are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) signal deteriorating labor market conditions, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through reduced consumer confidence.
failure_condition: SPX returns show no significant correlation with combined PAYEMS/UNRATE trends, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).
